skill.name: firecrawl
skill.description: Use Firecrawl to scrape pages, search the web, map sites, crawl URLs, extract structured data, and check crawl jobs.
skill.summary: Guides Firecrawl calls for web scraping, search, mapping, crawling, extraction, and job status checks.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown inline code, JSON examples, and frontmatter text. The real risk is network access: the skill documents Firecrawl operations that fetch, search, crawl, and extract content from external URLs.

medium_findings.title: External Web Crawling Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of Firecrawl modes that scrape, search, map, crawl, and extract data from URLs. This is expected behavior, but it can send target URLs, prompts, and extracted page content through an external web crawling service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented modes explicitly include scrape, search, map, crawl, and extract operations against HTTP or HTTPS URLs. The risk is inherent to the advertised network behavior, not hidden malicious code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md. The file contains Markdown inline code and fenced JSON examples, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review shows documentation examples only, with no script file or executable command invocation. The detected backticks are Markdown formatting.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at the frontmatter description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hash call, or encryption behavior appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a natural language description of web scraping and extraction features. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The file includes example URLs for Firecrawl documentation, example domains, and invalid URL demonstrations. They do not show credential exfiltration or unauthorized callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside usage examples and parameter validation guidance. No evidence found that the skill forces requests to attacker-controlled endpoints.

content.value_statement: Web research often needs clean page content, search results, and structured extraction from many URLs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to call Firecrawl modes with the correct nested parameters.
actual_capabilities: Shows the correct nested parameter format for Firecrawl tool calls.
Documents single-page scraping with markdown or HTML output formats.
Covers web search requests with query text and result limits.
Explains site mapping and asynchronous crawl job handling.
Provides structured extraction examples using prompts and schemas.
Shows how to check asynchronous job status by job identifier.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not implement Firecrawl itself.
Network results depend on Firecrawl service availability and access rules.
Crawl and extract modes are asynchronous and require later status checks.
Users must provide valid HTTP or HTTPS URLs in the expected fields.

use_cases.title: Collect Research Pages
use_cases.description: Scrape selected pages into readable markdown for review, summarization, or comparison.

use_cases.title: Map Website Structure
use_cases.description: Generate a site map before planning audits, migrations, or content inventories.

use_cases.title: Extract Article Details
use_cases.description: Pull titles, authors, publish times, and other fields from article pages.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl to scrape this page as markdown: [URL]. Return the main content and source URL.

prompt_templates.title: Search A Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl search for [topic]. Limit results to five and summarize the most relevant pages.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl A Small Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl crawl for [URL] with a limit of five pages. Save the job identifier and check status later.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Structured Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl extract on these URLs: [URLs]. Extract title, author, publish time, summary, and canonical URL.

output_examples.input: Scrape a documentation page and return markdown.
output_examples.output: A concise page title, source URL, main headings, and cleaned markdown content.

output_examples.input: Search for recent AI safety reports with a limit of five results.
output_examples.output: Five result summaries with page titles, URLs, and short relevance notes.

output_examples.input: Extract article metadata from a news URL.
output_examples.output: A structured summary containing the title, author, publish time, and article overview.

best_practices: Confirm that URLs are allowed to be crawled before making requests.
Use small crawl limits first to control cost, latency, and data volume.
Check asynchronous job status before assuming crawl or extract results are ready.
anti_patterns: Do not place Firecrawl parameters at the top level of the tool call.
Do not pass URLs without an HTTP or HTTPS scheme.
Do not crawl sensitive, private, or access-controlled pages without authorization.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure Firecrawl calls for scraping, search, mapping, crawling, extraction, and job status checks.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains only SKILL.md documentation and examples.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is publishable, but it guides external web crawling and extraction through Firecrawl.

faq.question: Can it extract structured data?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents extract mode with prompts and schemas for fields such as title and author.

faq.question: What should users avoid?
faq.answer: Users should avoid unauthorized crawling, invalid URL formats, and large crawls without a clear limit.
